tactical_shape: Functional, cautious approach with little risk or adventure
Used an offside trap and managed the game after taking the lead
standout_players: Luis Romo — hooked in the winning goal, his fifth international goal in his 64th appearance
Raúl Rangel — made a remarkable double save late on to preserve the lead
Raúl Jiménez — had a close-range effort blocked by Kim Seung-gyu
home_read.momentum: Two wins from two and through to the knockout stage, but the punditry says they have shown little beyond functional competence.

tactical_shape: Lacked urgency and adventure; long spells of passing without end product
Did not cope well with Mexico’s offside trap
standout_players: Kim Seung-gyu — made the error that gifted Mexico the goal; later produced a fine close-range block from Raúl Jiménez
Son Heung-min — struggled again, looked diminished and was substituted
fatigue_injuries: Son Heung-min — looks older than 33, struggling physically to cope with Mexico's offside trap
away_read.momentum: A disappointing defeat, with the mistake and limited showing leaving doubts about how far they can go, though a draw in the final game would still be enough to advance.
